page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 33 MB L3 cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong memory performance, with the PassMark memory score and large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 186.37 GB/sec both placing in the top 25% tier. Most compute benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core at 44554.69 and Geekbench multi-core at 10922.0, fall within the average performance tier. The server demonstrates stable throughput for static web serving at 24.87 GB/sec and handles Redis SET operations at 37.18 million operations per second. However, text generation performance for large language models is a notable limitation, registering in the bottom 25% tier for both small and large models.

From a resource-density perspective, the n2-standard-48 offers a balanced profile suitable for enterprise workloads that require high memory bandwidth and substantial multi-core processing without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs are externalized to persistent disks. This configuration is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.8 GHz across 24 physical cores, it features a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong memory bandwidth, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 186.37 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% tier. General compute, database operations, and web serving throughput fall into the average tier, while LLM text generation performance is relatively weak. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard enterprise workloads, offering a balanced resource density. It is best suited for database management, software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-48 is a general-purpose server featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB RAM. It delivers strong memory bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.

tagline: A balanced 48-vCPU general-purpose instance featuring strong memory bandwidth for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.

Strong memory performance with 186.37 GB/sec large-block read bandwidth.
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware accelerators.
Average performance for database operations and static web serving.
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and general-purpose workloads.
